prevalence.trend: Stable with slight increase in urban areas due to aging population and lifestyle changes

riskFactors: Unhealthy diet high in saturated fats
Socioeconomic factors like poverty and limited healthcare access
vulnerablePopulations: Low socioeconomic status groups with poor nutrition and stress
Urban populations exposed to pollution and fast-paced lifestyles
Indigenous and rural communities with limited screening
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Diagnosis based on clinical symptoms (angina), ECG abnormalities, non-invasive tests (stress test, echocardiography), and invasive coronary angiography; classified by severity using ESC/ACC guidelines adapted for Brazilian context.

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Aligned with SBC national guidelines, emphasizing risk stratification tools like SCORE adapted for Brazilian populations.
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Medical therapy (statins, antiplatelets), percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI), and coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG) available through SUS; emphasis on secondary prevention programs.
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Socioeconomic disparities leading to delayed diagnosis in North and Northeast regions; higher prevalence in South/Southeast due to urbanization and obesity epidemics; limited infrastructure in rural areas exacerbating access issues for low-income groups.
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Universal coverage via SUS providing free treatment; national initiatives like the Strategic Action Plan for Cardiovascular Health; strong cardiology networks in urban centers and growing telemedicine for remote areas.
